__form_note__: Export declarations come in radically different national forms (e.g., Chilean DUS, Turkish GUMRUK BEYANNAMESI, Chinese 报关单). Field positions, numbering, and labels differ. Hints below give the universal meaning of each field with all known label variants — match by meaning, not by exact field number.
export_declaration_no: Find the export declaration number — the main identifier in the document header. Labels: "Numero de identificacion" / "Numero Aceptacion" / "Customs export declaration No" / "ID/Reference No" / "Tescil No" / "Seri No" / "海关编号" / "预录入编号". The number may be a long digit string (16-18 digits) or a composite like "25610100EX00001697-26.03.2025" (number + date suffix). If two similar numbers are present (e.g., customs registration number and pre-entry number) — they usually match; prefer the registration/customs number. Keep the exact value (letters/digits/hyphens) and add to export_declaration_no.
export_declaration_date: Determine the declaration date. Labels: "FECHA" / "Fecha" / "Date" / "Yer ve Tarih" (Place and Date) / "申报日期" (Declaration date) / "出口日期" (Export date). Format varies: DD/MM/YYYY, DD.MM.YYYY, or YYYYMMDD without separators. If the document has multiple dates (declaration date, export date, technical "Aceptacion a tramite" / "Autorizacion de salida"), prioritize the main declaration/issue date. The date may also be embedded as a suffix in the declaration ID. Add to export_declaration_date.
invoice_no: Extract invoice number(s) from the invoice reference block. Labels: "Numero y fecha de Invoices" / "Factura de exportacion" / "FACTURA" / "Invoice" / "Fatura" — the reference may also be embedded inside a free-text "Additional information / Submitted documents" block (e.g., "Ek bilgi/sunulan belgeler") in the format "Invoice= [number] - [date]". Note: some declarations (notably Chinese 报关单) do NOT reference invoices at all — only a contract number. If the invoice is absent, leave empty. If multiple, join with "; ". Add to invoice_no.
invoice_date: Take invoice date(s) next to invoice number(s). The date typically follows the number after a dash or in a parallel "date" field. If multiple, join with "; ". Keep format as in document and add to invoice_date.
country_of_origin: If the export declaration explicitly shows the origin country, extract it. Labels: "Pais de origen" / "Country of origin" / "Mense Ulkesi" / "Mense ulke kodu" (numeric country code, e.g., "052" = Turkey) / "原产国(地区)". The field may be entirely ABSENT in some forms (Chilean DUS); if absent, leave empty (do not infer from exporter or port). Add to country_of_origin.
producer: If the producer is listed as "Producer" / "Manufacturer" / "Fabricante" / "Production/Sales Unit" / "生产销售单位" (often a separate field from the consignor/exporter, even if the values match in practice), extract the legal name. The plant approval number may appear separately (e.g., "Isletme Onay No = TR XX-XXXX") or as a suffix in the article code (e.g., "-F" suffix). If multiple, join with "; ". Add to producer.
buyer: Identify the buyer. Labels: "Consignatario" / "Consignee" / "Buyer" / "Alici" / "境外收货人" (Overseas Consignee). Keep the full name (and address if shown) in one line. Add to buyer.
seller: If the export declaration explicitly has "Seller" / "Supplier" / "Vendedor", extract it. Typically (in all standard national export declaration forms) the seller is NOT a separate field — it equals the exporter. Leave empty if not explicitly present. Add to seller.
exporter: Take the exporter. Labels: "Consignante o Exportador" / "Exporter" / "Gonderici" / "Ihracatci" (Sender/Exporter) / "境内发货人" (Domestic Consignor). Keep the full legal name (and address if shown) in one line and add to exporter.
incoterms: Extract Incoterms. Labels: "Clausula de venta" / "Clausula de Venta" / "Terms of sale" / "Teslim Sekli" / "成交方式". Format varies: just an abbreviation ("FOB"), abbreviation with place ("DAP MOSCOW"), or numeric code + abbreviation ("2 CFR" — extract only "CFR"). The place of loading/delivery may be in a separate port field, not next to the Incoterms code. Add to incoterms.
total_price: Take the overall total. Labels: "Total Valor FOB" / "Valor CIF" / "Valor Clausula de Venta" / "Doviz ve toplam fatura bedeli" / "总价". Important: take the total that matches the actual sale terms (the Incoterms of the deal). Do NOT default to "Valor FOB" if the deal Incoterms is CFR/CIF — use the corresponding sale-clause value instead. If no explicit total, sum line values. Keep currency and number format as in the document. Add to total_price.
proforma_no: If the declaration references a proforma ("Proforma Invoice" / "PI" / "Proforma No."), extract number(s). If multiple, join with "; ". Add to proforma_no.
HS_code: Collect all tariff codes from line items. Labels: "Cod. Arancel" / "Arancel" / "HS Code" / "Tariff code" / "Esya Kodu" / "商品编号". Format varies widely: 8 digits without separators ("03031420"), 6 digits with dots + 2+2 digits with spaces ("0303.14.20 00 00"), 10 digits without separators ("0303899007"), or standard with dots ("0304.81.20"). Record unique codes in one list separated by "; ", keeping document format. Add to HS_code.
products: The "products" block is expandable: create product_1, product_2, ... for each line item (ITEM 1, ITEM 2, ...) in the goods description section. Keep order from the document. Fill all available fields for each position; if a shared attribute is given once (e.g., one Latin species), duplicate it in the relevant product_i. In token_refs include references to source text/table fragments used.
products[*].name_product: Take the commercial description. Labels: "Descripcion de mercancias" / "Description of goods" / "Nombre" / "Ticari Tanim" (Commercial name) / "商品名称" (Product name). The description may be split across multiple sub-fields with prefixes like "Atrib.1, Atrib.2, ..." — concatenate them into one line. An article code at the start (e.g., "HGTPRUS2569") is a separate ID, not part of the name. The description may be in a non-Latin script (Chinese, Cyrillic) or in the local language (Turkish, Spanish). Join multi-line into one line. Add to products[i].name_product.
products[*].latin_name: Latin species name in format "Genus species" (binomial nomenclature, two uppercase words). Locations vary: in parentheses after the commercial name, as a separate line labeled "拉丁名:" / "Latin name:" / similar, or split across multiple sub-fields/Atrib. lines inside the description (Genus and species may be separated by other text — extract any matching binomial pattern even if discontinuous). If none, leave empty.
products[*].size_product: Extract size/grade (e.g., "Calibre 3-4 LBS", "1.3-1.8 KG", "80/120", "CALIBRE 6-9 LB"). Multiple sizes may be enumerated within one line (e.g., "1-3KG 3-6KG 6-10KG 10KG+") — these are ALL sizes of one aggregated position, do not split into separate products[i]. Keep as printed and add to products[i].size_product.
products[*].unit_box: Take the unit of measure from the corresponding column. Labels: "U. Medida" / "Unit" / similar. Note: in export declarations the unit is typically a WEIGHT unit ("KG" / "KN" = kilos netos / "千克" = kg / "KGM"), NOT a packaging unit (CTN/BOX/BAG) like in invoices. The price below is per kilogram, not per package. Add to products[i].unit_box.
products[*].packages: State number of packages. Labels: "Number and kind of packages" / "Cantidad de bultos" / "Cases/Cartons" / "Kap Adedi" / "件数" / "DESCRIPCION DE BULTOS" (Chilean section below goods, format "[N] [type]" e.g., "880 CAJACARTON"). Note: in export declarations packages are typically given at DOCUMENT level, not per item; if there are multiple items, packages is usually NOT split per item. If packing type is shown, keep number and type in one line (e.g., "1375 CAJAS"). Add to products[i].packages.
products[*].price_per_unit: Take unit price. Labels: "Precio unitario FOB" / "Unit price" / "Precio.Unit/Fob" / "单价" / similar. Decimal separator may be comma (e.g., "6,801818"). In some forms, an explicit unit price is ABSENT — only a line total is present (e.g., "Kalem fiyati" in Turkish forms); in that case compute as "line total / net weight" or leave empty. Keep currency and number format (dot/comma) as in the document. Add to products[i].price_per_unit.
products[*].total_price: Take the line total. Labels: "Valor FOB" (per item) / "Kalem fiyati" / "总价" / similar. If no explicit value, compute as quantity x unit price, keeping currency and format. Add to products[i].total_price.
products[*].net_weight: Extract net weight per line. Labels: "Peso Neto" / "Net weight" / "KN" (kilos netos) / "Net Agirlik" / "净重(千克)". May be at item level OR at document level (when only one position exists, the net weight in the cumulative field is the line value). If only document-level value is available with multiple items, duplicate it in each products[i] only when justified. Keep number and unit as in the document. Add to products[i].net_weight.
products[*].gross_weight: Extract gross weight per line. Labels: "Peso Bruto" / "Gross weight" / "Brut Agirlik" / "毛重(千克)". Keep number format and unit. Add to products[i].gross_weight.
